title: Rootless containers - /dev/ttyACM0 permission denied
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Local serial device /dev/ttyACM0 cannot be accessed from container, despite passing `--userns=keep-id` and `--group-add keep groups`.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Ensure user part of group with access to the device

2. Run container with
```
[zigbee2mqtt@pi2-zigbee2mqtt ~]$ podman run --userns=keep-id --group-add keep-groups -it -v $(pwd)/data:/app/data:Z -v /run/udev:/run/udev:ro --device=/dev/ttyACM0:/dev/ttyACM0 ubuntu bash
zigbee2mqtt@79e47cba46a0:/$ cat /dev/ttyACM0
cat: /dev/ttyACM0: Permission denied
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Cannot access /dev/ttyACM0 from within the container.
```
cat: /dev/ttyACM0: Permission denied
zigbee2mqtt@79e47cba46a0:/$ id
uid=1001(zigbee2mqtt) gid=1001(zigbee2mqtt) groups=1001(zigbee2mqtt),65534(nogroup)
zigbee2mqtt@79e47cba46a0:/$ ls -lan /dev/
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 5     0     0    380 Oct 19 12:32 .
dr-xr-xr-x. 1     0     0     39 Oct 19 12:32 ..
crw--w----. 1  1001     5 136, 0 Oct 19 12:34 console
lrwxrwxrwx. 1     0     0     11 Oct 19 12:32 core -> /proc/kcore
lrwxrwxrwx. 1     0     0     13 Oct 19 12:32 fd -> /proc/self/fd
crw-rw-rw-. 1 65534 65534   1, 7 Aug  6 00:00 full
drwxrwxrwt. 2     0     0     40 Oct 19 12:32 mqueue
crw-rw-rw-. 1 65534 65534   1, 3 Aug  6 00:00 null
lrwxrwxrwx. 1     0     0      8 Oct 19 12:32 ptmx -> pts/ptmx
drwxr-xr-x. 2     0     0      0 Oct 19 12:32 pts
crw-rw-rw-. 1 65534 65534   1, 8 Aug  6 00:00 random
drwxrwxrwt. 2     0     0     40 Oct 19 12:32 shm
lrwxrwxrwx. 1     0     0     15 Oct 19 12:32 stderr -> /proc/self/fd/2
lrwxrwxrwx. 1     0     0     15 Oct 19 12:32 stdin -> /proc/self/fd/0
lrwxrwxrwx. 1     0     0     15 Oct 19 12:32 stdout -> /proc/self/fd/1
crw-rw-rw-. 1 65534 65534   5, 0 Oct 19 11:32 tty
crw-rw----. 1 65534 65534 166, 0 Oct 19 12:31 ttyACM0
crw-rw-rw-. 1 65534 65534   1, 9 Aug  6 00:00 urandom
crw-rw-rw-. 1 65534 65534   1, 5 Aug  6 00:00 zero
zigbee2mqtt@79e47cba46a0:/$
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Process within the container is able to access ttyACM0.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Running on fedora 34 on raspberry pi.

The actual container that I'm trying to run is `docker.io/koenkk/zigbee2mqtt:latest`, which fails with:

```
[zigbee2mqtt@pi2-zigbee2mqtt ~]$ podman run --userns=keep-id --group-add keep-groups -it -v $(pwd)/data:/app/data:Z -v /run/udev:/run/udev:ro --device=/dev/ttyACM0 docker.io/koenkk/zigbee2mqtt:latest
Using '/app/data' as data directory
Zigbee2MQTT:info  2021-10-19 12:37:14: Logging to console and directory: '/app/data/log/2021-10-19.12-37-08' filename: log.txt
Zigbee2MQTT:info  2021-10-19 12:37:14: Starting Zigbee2MQTT version 1.21.2 (commit #4492212
)
Zigbee2MQTT:info  2021-10-19 12:37:14: Starting zigbee-herdsman (0.13.149)
CREATED DECONZ ADAPTER
Zigbee2MQTT:error 2021-10-19 12:37:16: Error while starting zigbee-herdsman
Zigbee2MQTT:error 2021-10-19 12:37:16: Failed to start zigbee
Zigbee2MQTT:error 2021-10-19 12:37:16: Check https://www.zigbee2mqtt.io/information/FAQ.html#help-zigbee2mqtt-fails-to-start for possible solutions
Zigbee2MQTT:error 2021-10-19 12:37:16: Exiting...
Zigbee2MQTT:error 2021-10-19 12:37:16: Error: Error while opening serialport 'Error: Error: Permission denied, cannot open /dev/ttyACM0'
    at SerialPort.<anonymous> (/app/node_modules/zigbee-herdsman/src/adapter/deconz/driver/driver.ts:182:28)
    at SerialPort._error (/app/node_modules/zigbee-herdsman/node_modules/@serialport/stream/lib/index.js:198:14)
    at /app/node_modules/zigbee-herdsman/node_modules/@serialport/stream/lib/index.js:242:12
[zigbee2mqtt@pi2-zigbee2mqtt ~]$
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[zigbee2mqtt@pi2-zigbee2mqtt ~]$ podman --version
podman version 3.4.0
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
[zigbee2mqtt@pi2-zigbee2mqtt ~]$ rpm -q podman
podman-3.4.0-1.fc34.armv7hl
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Running on fedora 34 on a raspberry pi

comments.body:
Does the user running the container on the host have access to `/dev/ttyACM0` outside the container? If not, Podman won't be able to access it. Rootless containers inherit all the security restrictions of the user running them, so if the user has no access to the device, neither do we.

If the user in question does have access to the device it could be via a supplementary group. If so, `--group-add=keep-groups` can be added to your `podman run` command line to cause these groups to be preserved in the container.

The user has access to `/dev/ttyACM0` outside of the container, I have confirmed that the application will start up outside of container without issue.

As stated in my initial post, I _am_ running the command with `--group-add=keep-groups` and `--userns=keep-id`.

Any further ideas?

Can you provide the output of `ls -al /dev/ttyACM0` on the host, not in the container?

Also, can you try disabling SELinux in the container (`--security-opt label=disable` on the `podman run` command line)?

since this issue had no feedback for more than a week, I am closing it.

Please reopen if you've more details
Had the same issue, `--security-opt label=disable` fixed it.
